suppliers.name: Abbott Diabetes Care
products: FreeStyle Libre 3 — being phased out in the UK by end of March 2026 per Diabetes UK's own announcement
FreeStyle Libre 3 Plus — current flagship sensor (15-day wear, real-time CGM), what the NHS will offer going forward
FreeStyle Libre 2 Plus — remains available for self-funding customers after the NHS moves fully to 3 Plus

frameworks.name: NHS Supply Chain — Insulin Pumps, CGM & Hybrid Closed Loop
frameworks.note: 12 suppliers incl. Abbott, Dexcom, Insulet, Medtronic

alerts: UK entity is Abbott Diabetes Care Limited (Companies House 02548046, Witney), formerly MediSense (U.K.) Holding Ltd, renamed 2005 following Abbott's 2004 acquisition of TheraSense/MediSense. Not found on NHS Supply Chain (searched Libre, FreeStyle Libre, Abbott Diabetes — zero results) — confirms FreeStyle Libre is dispensed via NHS prescription (Drug Tariff), not the hospital consumables catalogue. Compatible with third-party closed-loop systems (mylife CamAPS FX/YpsoPump) per Abbott's own technical documentation.
suppliers.note: E&T client. FreeStyle Libre 2 Plus / 3 Plus sensors, LibreView. On the Insulin Pumps, CGM & HCL framework (2025/S 000-4043) to 31/05/2027, ~£1bn/4yr.

voice.line: Lead with patient outcomes and the cost of poor glucose control — clinical evidence first, then whole-system savings (fewer admissions, less staff time). Not a price play.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: Framework-verified: 'Insulin Pumps, Continuous Glucose Monitoring, Products Contributing to the Delivery of Hybrid Closed Loop Pathways and Associated Products' (2025/S 000-4043, Medical and Surgical Consumables) — Abbott Laboratories Ltd named among 12 awarded suppliers. NOT tagged Pathology and laboratory: Abbott Laboratories Ltd / Abbott Rapid Diagnostics Ltd / Abbott Toxicology Ltd are awarded on the Lab Diagnostics/POCT framework, but those read as separate Abbott business divisions from this specific 'Abbott Diabetes Care' supplier record, whose catalogued products are diabetes-only. FreeStyle Optium Neo confirmed via NHS dm+d as a Drug Tariff item — a different route (community prescribing), not NHSSC — noted, not tagged as a separate speciality.

frameworks.name: NHS Supply Chain — Laboratory Diagnostics, POCT & Pathology (2023/S 000-028831, to 2028)
frameworks.note: named supplier

alerts: Removed 'SureStep' — this appears to be a LifeScan brand (originally Johnson & Johnson, now Platinum Equity-owned), not an Abbott product; no evidence found that Abbott ever marketed it. Current NHS point-of-care glucose testing under Abbott runs via FreeStyle Precision Pro/i-STAT instead. Diagnostics products span two UK entities: Abbott Laboratories Limited (00329102, Maidenhead, trades as 'Abbott Diagnostics' — Alinity/Architect/i-STAT) and Abbott Rapid Diagnostics Ltd (01716581, also Maidenhead — Afinion/ID NOW/Determine/Actim distribution). Both confirmed distinct from Abbott Medical U.K. Ltd (cardiac/EP) and Abbott Diabetes Care Ltd (FreeStyle Libre), already recorded separately in this dataset.

voice.line: Single harmonised Alinity platform across disciplines with strong POC crossover.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: added Blood and transfusion for Alinity s-series (donor blood/plasma screening -- HBsAg, anti-HCV, anti-HBc, HTLV I/II, Chagas) -- already bundled into the 'Alinity ci / h / s / m' product line but had no matching speciality tag; this family genuinely sits apart from general pathology, likely procured directly by NHS Blood and Transplant rather than the general NHS Supply Chain catalogue. Added BinaxNOW/Determine/Actim/SureStep -- the former Alere rapid-diagnostics range (Abbott acquired Alere Oct 2017), confirmed live on NHS Supply Chain under a distinct legal entity 'ABBOTT RAPID DIAGNOSTICS LTD' (separate from 'ABBOTT LABORATORIES LIMITED (BERKS)', which covers i-STAT/ARCHITECT/Alinity) -- kept as one combined 'Abbott Diagnostics' record since both are diagnostics-not-diabetes, even though NHS Supply Chain itself treats them as two legal suppliers. CONFIRMED clean separation from Abbott Diabetes Care: FreeStyle Libre is supplied via the NHS Drug Tariff (Part IXR)/community prescribing, an entirely different UK procurement route from the hospital Supply Chain catalogue used by this record -- no overlap or double-counting risk between the two supplier records. ARCHITECT is being gradually migrated to Alinity ci (both still live on NHS Supply Chain today) -- described as a managed transition, not a discontinuation, don't flag ARCHITECT as withdrawn.

alerts: Physidia (home dialysis machine) is manufactured by a French company, Physidia — B. Braun Avitum is its exclusive UK distributor, not the manufacturer; worth noting the distinction. UK entity B. Braun Medical Limited (Companies House 02296559, Sheffield) confirmed family-controlled at UK level; ultimate parent B. Braun Melsungen AG (Germany) remains privately held and family-controlled (CEO Anna Maria Braun, 6th generation), no public listing. B. Braun SE fully acquired True Digital Surgery (Sept 2025, digital 3D robotic surgical microscopy), feeding directly into the already-listed Aesculap Aeos platform. US FDA recalls in 2025-2026 (Lactated Ringer's/Sodium Chloride particulate matter, Microbore Extension Set mislabelling) are US-market actions — no equivalent UK/MHRA recall was found, so these should not be presented as a UK-market issue without separate confirmation.

voice.line: Sells on staff/patient safety (passive needlestick protection) and total system integration — "Sharing Expertise", not price.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: Multi-speciality review 20/07/2026: Obstetric epidural / regional anaesthesia supplies. | Renal framework note: the actual contracting entity on NHS Supply Chain's Renal Replacement Therapies framework is 'B. Braun Avitum UK Limited' (Sheffield) - B. Braun's dedicated renal/dialysis subsidiary, running 9 UK dialysis centres and the Dialog+ machine. Same corporate family as this record. | Continence & urology: confirmed awarded supplier on the Urology and Bowel Management framework. | Product range substantially expanded 20/07/2026. Confirmed genuine gaps: Theatres/surgical (Aesculap sutures/implants/energy devices/neurosurgery, all NPC-coded live on NHS Supply Chain), Wound care (Askina range, Prontosan), Infection prevention and PPE (Softalind/Softaskin/Softasept skin cleansing range). DELIBERATELY NOT tagged Ostomy and stoma care: searched NHS Supply Chain directly for B. Braun ostomy products, found none — B. Braun's Emerald service (bbraun-emerald.co.uk) is a specialist continence/urology/stoma nursing and home-dispensing SERVICE that supplies OTHER manufacturers' NHS-approved appliances, not a B. Braun-manufactured ostomy product line. Correction: Perseus/Atlan anaesthesia workstation is a DRÄGER product, not B. Braun — was never listed here, flagged to avoid future mis-attribution. Structural note: B. Braun's own site now shows three divisions (Hospital Care, Aesculap, Avitum), not four — the former OPM division's continence/wound/ostomy lines are now folded under the Avitum name.

alerts.text: Bausch + Lomb Corporation (NYSE: BLCO) began trading independently on 06/05/2022 following an IPO separating it from Bausch Health Companies Inc. -- but this is NOT a full spin-off: Bausch Health retains ~88% of B+L's outstanding shares (as of 11/02/2026 filings). A 2024-25 process to take B+L fully private via third-party sale was explored and did NOT result in a transaction. Publicly listed and operationally independent, but financially not yet fully separated -- a live, evolving situation.

Progressa+ (next-gen ICU bed) launched in the US June 2023 — a specific UK launch date could not be confirmed. Two other Hill-Rom UK entities are dissolved (01702696, 08394146) — don't use those numbers.

Domain now redirects to hillrom.baxter.eu/en-GB. A second research pass could not confirm The Vest Airway Clearance System/MetaNeb/Volara as currently UK-marketed (absent from both NHS Supply Chain and the current Baxter/Hillrom UK category list) — treat respiratory-line UK availability as uncertain pending direct confirmation, despite it being listed as a product above from an earlier pass.

Hillrom -- part of Baxter International since Dec 2021; see also the Baxter Healthcare Ltd record for IV therapy/renal/surgical lines.

Welch Allyn (264 NHS Supply Chain results) and Liko are strongly NHS-confirmed; hospital beds (Centrella/Progressa/TotalCare) and respiratory (Vest/Monarch) NOT found on NHS Supply Chain -- company-site-confirmed only, likely capital/specialist-homecare procurement routes.

alerts: Bayer AG sold a ~€3bn (~$3.4bn) minority, non-controlling stake in its long-acting reversible contraceptives business (Mirena, Kyleena, Jaydess specifically named) to Apollo Global Management, announced 10/07/2026; Bayer retains majority ownership and full operational control, deal expected to close Q3 2026 subject to antitrust approval — reported rationale is raising capital toward Bayer's Roundup litigation costs. UK trading entity is Bayer plc (Companies House 00935048) — a separate, dissolved 'Bayer UK Limited' (04478534) should not be confused with it. Angeliq's UK stock status is uncertain: a patient forum reports discontinuation, but its marketing authorisation was renewed 14/07/2025 and the SmPC revised as recently as 07/04/2026 — may be a supply shortage rather than discontinuation; verify directly before stating current availability.

alerts.title: Class 2 Medicines Recall: ChloraPrep 1mL applicators, EL(26)A/34 (13/07/2026) — the fourth ChloraPrep recall since May 2025
alerts.detail: Recalled 13/07/2026 for a potential breach of sterility in the packaging process (all lots with expiry up to 05/2029); contamination "could lead to increased infection rates". Follows EL(26)A/26 (02/06/2026, 1mL and Frepp 1.5mL, same sterility reason) and two 2025 recalls for open applicator seals (EL(25)A/22, EL(25)A/36).
alerts.use: If ChloraPrep is the incumbent, continuity of licensed supply is the conversation — a licensed second source de-risks the category.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: Multi-speciality review 20/07/2026: Vacutainer / Eclipse / Safety-Lok specimen collection, BACTEC blood culture. | Renal and dialysis: confirmed awarded supplier on the Renal Replacement Therapies framework (vascular access / dialysis catheter components). 20/07/2026 batch: added 'Patient monitoring' for BD's Advanced Patient Monitoring division (Swan-Ganz, FloTrac, HemoSphere) — confirmed genuine, completed acquisition of Edwards Lifesciences' Critical Care group Sept 2024 ($4.2bn); this is a real, current BD business unit with no prior tag home. Diabetes Care (Nano/AutoShield/Ultra-Fine/Micro-Fine+ pen needles) DELIBERATELY NOT added under BD: this business spun off as the independent public company Embecta Corp on 01/04/2022. NHS Supply Chain's catalogue still lists these SKUs under 'BECTON DICKINSON UK LTD' (supplier-attribution lag), and 'Embecta' returns zero NHS SC results, so this is a genuine open question — flagging for Lou to decide rather than guessing which name to file it under. Also NOT added: Angio-Seal (confirmed a Terumo product, not BD/Bard) and Macroplastique/Contigen (Macroplastique is now Laborie-owned; Contigen discontinued 2011) — both were checked specifically because they're commonly mis-attributed to BD/Bard. BD Whitacre spinal/epidural needles confirmed genuine but have no clean speciality fit in the current vocabulary (anaesthesia-adjacent) — left untagged rather than forced. Pharmaceutical Systems (Hylok/Neopak/Vystra/Hypak) confirmed genuine but is a B2B OEM component business sold to pharma manufacturers, not NHS trusts — out of scope for an NHS-facing sales tool, not added.

FDA/Health Canada Class 2 recall (Feb 2026) for DxC 700 AU and AU5800 — a software fault (Microsoft SQL Server 2022 Express-related) that can halt analysis and delay reporting; a matching MHRA/UK-specific notice could not be confirmed — flag as unverified for the UK market specifically. No NHS Supply Chain listing — expected, lab analysers of this scale are typically on managed-service/leasing contracts.

voice.line: DxA 5000 full-track automation and high-sensitivity assays for consolidated blood-sciences hubs.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: added DxFLEX (CE-IVD 13-colour clinical flow cytometer, validated for leukaemia/lymphoma immunophenotyping) -- distinct from CytoFLEX, which is a RESEARCH-only instrument under the separate beckman.com Life Sciences brand, not equivalent clinical-grade, correctly not added here. CONFIRMED: ACL/coagulation products are NOT Beckman Coulter's -- that's Werfen's (Instrumentation Laboratory), whose own supplier-seed.json record already correctly lists ACL TOP; Beckman Coulter's old exclusive distribution agreement for hemostasis systems with IL ended 22/06/2012. No cross-contamination found between the two records -- verified clean. Beckman Coulter's DxN VERIS molecular diagnostics platform was discontinued around 2020-2021 (Danaher standardised on Cepheid's GeneXpert instead) -- was never listed here, nothing to remove, noted for awareness only. All Beckman Coulter capital lab platforms confirmed absent from the NHS Supply Chain catalogue (procured via direct trust/managed-service contracts) -- DxA 5000 real-world NHS use independently confirmed via Beckman's own case study (Worcestershire Royal Hospital / Worcestershire Acute Hospitals NHS Trust).

Exhaustively tested the NHS Supply Chain catalogue for all four families (Bio-Rad, Liquichek, Lyphochek, D-10, VARIANT, BioPlex, IH-500, blood grouping reagent) -- zero matches for any Bio-Rad brand, though the catalogue does index this product type generally (one Roche Cobas HbA1c reagent found via a generic search) -- treat the whole range as company-site-confirmed only, not NHS-Supply-Chain-catalogue-confirmed.

alerts: IMPORTANT SAFETY FLAG: the ACCOLADE pacemaker/CRT-P family (Accolade, Proponent, Essentio, Altura 2, Visionist, Valitude) is subject to an FDA Class I recall/correction affecting over 1 million devices — a high battery-impedance issue causing an unexpected 'Safety Mode'; as of 18 March 2026 Boston Scientific reported 4 deaths and 2,557 serious injuries. The fix is a software update (SMR6, released March 2026), not device explant — worth surfacing to any rep positioning this pacemaker family. Separately, the Carotid Wallstent Monorail Endoprosthesis was recalled in 2025 for a manufacturing defect (narrow inner lumen risking stroke). UK entity: Boston Scientific Limited (Companies House 02729626, London) — a differently-numbered 'Boston Scientific (UK) Limited' (04949633) is dissolved since Oct 2023, don't use it. Boston Scientific has an active pending $14.5bn acquisition of Penumbra, Inc. (mechanical thrombectomy) announced 2026 but not yet closed — don't add Penumbra products until completion is confirmed. Other recent acquisitions (Cortex cardiac mapping, Bolt Medical, Nalu Medical, SoundCath, Intera Oncology) are agreed/closing through 2025-2026 but not yet reflected as flagship UK-commercial products.

voice.line: Leader in LAA closure (WATCHMAN) and fast-scaling pulsed-field ablation — the EP/structural innovation play.

Deduplicated Ranger DCB/Eluvia peripheral stent (same product as 'Eluvia / Ranger (peripheral)') and the repeated WATCHMAN FLX/FARAPULSE lines. Added 'Oncology and infusion' for the Interventional Oncology/embolization line (TheraSphere, DC Bead/LC Bead, cryoablation, coils) — confirmed on bostonscientific.com, fits the confirmed Endoscopy/Endourology/Oncology Ablation framework but was entirely unrepresented before this batch. Added 'Continence & urology' — Rezum (BPH) and SpaceOAR (prostate radiotherapy spacer) are male/prostate urology products, not Women's health/maternity (that tag is correctly kept for Bulkamid, a female urethral bulking agent). Not added: full CRM lead/mapping-hardware catalogue (kept to headline product families) and AMS penile prosthesis/AUS range (Urology division confirmed genuine on bd.com-style sourcing but not yet cross-checked against an NHSSC framework in this pass).

frameworks.name: NHS Supply Chain — Renal Replacement Therapies (2023/S 000-017117, to 2028)
frameworks.note: named supplier — verify lot at source

alerts.text: Re-tagged primary speciality to Anaesthesia and critical care (kept Renal and dialysis as a secondary tag) -- CE-marked indications are cytokine/bilirubin/myoglobin adsorption in critical illness and intraoperative anticoagulant removal during cardiac surgery, not renal replacement itself (it's commonly run in-line with a CRRT circuit, which is a delivery mechanism, not the clinical indication). NICE has published three Medtech Innovation Briefings -- MIB87 (sepsis), MIB217 (COVID-19 respiratory failure), MIB249 (cardiac surgery bleeding reduction) -- all note the evidence base is low-quality/small-sample/mostly observational; current sepsis evidence does not show improved mortality. Any efficacy claims should be qualified as emerging, not established.

Do NOT list DrugSorb-ATR (ticagrelor removal — FDA De Novo denied April 2025, unclear CE status) or HemoDefend-RBC/-BGA (blood-transfusion safety filters) as available products -- both are investigational/pipeline-stage only, not approved anywhere for the DrugSorb-ATR/HemoDefend line.
MERGED with a duplicate 'CytoSorbents Medical UK Ltd' record listing the same core CytoSorb cartridge under 'Renal and dialysis' only.

voice.line: The blood-purification adjunct for sepsis/critical-care nephrology — adds a therapy layer onto CRRT.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: expanded from 1 to 4 named products, merged a duplicate record, re-tagged primary speciality to critical care. Confirmed genuine entity CytoSorbents Medical UK Limited (Companies House 13992244).

frameworks.name: NHS Supply Chain — Clinical & Sharps Waste
frameworks.note: named supplier — verify lot at source

alerts.text: The domain danielshealth.co.uk is a PARKED/inactive domain, not Daniels Health's genuine UK site -- the real UK operating entity trades as 'Sharpsmart' at sharpsmart.co.uk (Companies House: SHARPSMART LIMITED, 04261387). Exhaustively checked NHS Supply Chain: 'Sharpsmart', 'Clinismart' and 'Bladesmart' ALL return zero results -- these appear to run as managed-lease service contracts direct with trusts (Daniels owns/reprocesses the containers, trusts pay a service fee) rather than catalogue-orderable disposables, which is the most likely explanation for the absence, though this is inference not confirmed fact. FLAG: 'Sharpsguard' (already listed in this record) could not be confirmed as a genuine Daniels Health/Sharpsmart product in this research pass -- it did not appear anywhere on sharpsmart.co.uk. Worth checking directly, as this name is commonly associated with a different manufacturer -- do not present it as confirmed Daniels/Sharpsmart branding without verification.

IMPORTANT: there is a SEPARATE, unrelated company also trading in sharps/clinical waste containers under a very similar name -- 'Daniels Healthcare' at daniels.co.uk, part of Mauser Packaging Solutions since 2016 (Mauser UK Ltd). Its products (e.g. 'Daniels Transport 7') ARE confirmed live on NHS Supply Chain under supplier 'MAUSER UK LTD' -- do not conflate the two companies; Mauser's Daniels Healthcare does not offer Sharpsmart's reusable-container model.

voice.line: Reusable, sealed sharps systems that cut sharps injuries and waste cost/carbon — sustainability angle.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: added Clinismart and Bladesmart -- confirmed genuine current Sharpsmart brand names on sharpsmart.co.uk. Corrected the domain reference. No 'Cliniwaste' brand found anywhere -- Clinismart is the correct name for the clinical/infectious waste container range. Flagged 'Sharpsguard' (already in this record) as unverified/possibly a different manufacturer's brand -- see alerts[].

alerts.text: Byte (direct-to-consumer clear aligners) was voluntarily suspended 24/10/2024 amid FDA regulatory review, and Dentsply Sirona confirmed in January 2025 it would no longer offer Byte as a D2C product -- do not list Byte as a current product.

DENTSPLY International and Sirona Dental Systems completed an all-stock merger 29/02/2016 forming Dentsply Sirona -- 'Sirona' is not an independent company and survives only as part of product sub-brand names (Sinius, Intego, Axano). The old Companies House entity SIRONA DENTAL SYSTEMS LIMITED (04893127) was dissolved 17/03/2020. The live UK operating entity is DENTSPLY IH LIMITED (01480123, Stonehouse, Gloucestershire) trading as 'Dentsply Sirona GB'.

Do not confuse the dental chair brand 'Intego' with Bayer/Medrad's 'Intego' contrast-media injector consumables -- both appear under similar search terms but are unrelated products from different companies.

2025-2026: Dentsply Sirona reviewed strategic alternatives for Wellspect Healthcare (bladder/bowel care -- unrelated to dental) then retained it (Sept 2025). Separately announced a restructuring plan targeting ~$120m savings and a $144m impairment on orthodontic/implant solutions; dividend eliminated. No divestiture of the dental imaging (Orthophos/Xios) business found -- an earlier-looking filing about 'XRAY' refers to the company's NASDAQ ticker, not an X-ray/imaging division sale.

voice.line: Only on-framework vendor spanning consumables, implants, imaging and chairside CAD/CAM.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: added ProTaper/WaveOne (endodontics), Ceram.X/Prime&Bond (restoratives -- corrected from an earlier brief's unverifiable 'Prime dental composite', the real products are Ceram.X composite and Prime&Bond active adhesive, both confirmed live on NHS Supply Chain), MIS Implants (a genuine Dentsply-owned value-segment implant brand, acquired 2016 for $375m, distinct from the premium Astra Tech/Ankylos lines), and SureSmile (confirmed UK-relevant via a dedicated en-gb site and UK trade press, but private/direct-to-practice only -- no evidence of NHS offering). Confirmed Byte is discontinued (see alerts). Astra Tech/Ankylos implants, CEREC/Primescan CAD/CAM and Orthophos imaging (all already listed) remain genuine current products but confirmed absent from the general NHS Supply Chain consumables catalogue -- consistent with capital/implant equipment being procured via a different route; this record's separate 'Dentsply IH Ltd' entry (NHS Supply Chain Dental Technologies framework, Lots 1-3) is the correct place to look for the actual awarded-lot detail, kept as its own record per its existing sourced note.20/07/2026: MERGED two duplicate records into this one -- 'Sirona Dental Systems' and 'Dentsply IH Ltd' were both separately-listed generic-lot entries for the same company (Sirona hasn't been independent since 2016; Dentsply IH Ltd is simply the UK legal entity behind the Dentsply Sirona brand). Added WaveOne Gold, Dycal, Aquasil, ProRinse, Luxator (all NHS-Supply-Chain-confirmed) and Primescan/Xios/Sinius-Intego-Axano (company-site only, capital equipment not on the general catalogue).

Confirmed on NHS Supply Chain, DePuy Synthes' product listings run under a distinct supplier name ('DEPUY SYNTHES UK - DEPUY EDIRECT') from Johnson & Johnson MedTech's own listings, matching this dataset's existing decision to keep them as two separate supplier records.

Two separate active UK entities exist — Draeger Medical UK Limited (04310199, Hemel Hempstead, the correct entity for this hospital/medical product range) and Draeger Safety UK Ltd (00777464, Blyth, Northumberland, a different business line — industrial gas detection/PPE/drug-and-alcohol testing) — don't conflate the two; Blyth is the larger, more publicly visible UK site so is the address most likely to get pulled in by mistake. alerts.text: MAJOR CORPORATE CHANGE: Exactech, Inc. filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy in October 2024, driven by ~2,600 patient lawsuits over a packaging/oxidation defect (vacuum bags missing an oxygen barrier layer, causing polyethylene insert oxidation across 140,000+ knee, ~130,000 hip and ~1,500 ankle systems, plus the Equinoxe shoulder system) -- recall ran August 2021 through April 2024. Court confirmed a restructuring plan 15/09/2025; assets sold to a new investor group (Strategic Value Partners, Stellex Capital Management, Greywolf Capital Management). The business relaunched as ADVITA ORTHO, LLC on 03/11/2025, which now owns and distributes the Equinoxe/Vantage/Alteon/Truliant/Novation/Newton/GPS product lines. TPG Capital (the brief's assumed current owner) no longer owns the business -- a litigation trust was set up to pursue claims against TPG. UK legal entity Companies House record shows 'ADVITA ORTHO UK LIMITED' with 'previous name: EXACTECH (UK) LIMITED'. NHS Supply Chain now refers to the supplier as 'Advita Ortho (Exactech) Ltd.' Recall litigation/restructuring fallout is still live as of the most recent sources -- do not present the recall as resolved-and-behind-them without this context.
alerts.source: SEC/court filings / medtechdive.com / prnewswire.com / Companies House

(Invacare Europe & APAC -- covers this UK entity) and Direct Healthcare Group (DHG) on 30/01/2026, merging them into a new combined company 'DHCare'; ARCHIMED (DHG's former backer) retains a minority stake. This is separate from, and post-dates, Invacare Corporation's 2023 US Chapter 11 -- the US lineage kept only North America, which was itself sold on again (to MIGA Holdings, Nov 2024). Invacare UK (Pencoed, Wales) is now part of DHCare, not the original US Invacare Corporation. No visible rebrand on invacare.co.uk as of this check -- still 'Invacare GB' branding, likely mid-transition. DHG's brands (Dyna-Form, System RoMedic, Raizer) are now sister brands under the same DHCare parent, no longer independent competitors.

alerts: IMPORTANT SAFETY FLAG: the Impella heart pump range (via the Abiomed acquisition) has had a serious run of FDA Class I recalls through 2025 — Automated Impella Controller connectivity issues (linked to 3 patient deaths, corrective actions in July/Sept/Oct x2/Dec 2025), purge cassette leaks, differential pressure sensor malfunctions, and an introducer kit thrombosis risk alert. The device isn't withdrawn from market (corrections are software-update/corrective-action in nature), but this is material and currently unflagged — worth surfacing before positioning Impella with a clinician. UK entity confirmed: Johnson & Johnson Medical Limited (Companies House SC132162, Scotland, formerly Ethicon Limited). No overlap or conflict found with DePuy Synthes (J&J MedTech) or Johnson & Johnson Surgical Vision, both separate records in this dataset — the DePuy Synthes 18-24 month spin-off (announced Oct 2025) remains on track and unchanged. The reported Cerenovus (neurovascular) sale exploration (~$1-1.5bn, FT/Reuters) remains just that — no completed sale found; treat Cerenovus as still J&J-owned but under a cautious flag.

alerts: Ownership/governance note: Sybill Storz — daughter of founder Karl Storz, long-standing managing director then supervisory board chair — died 28 November 2025, aged 88, reflected in a recent Companies House PSC filing change. Her sister Gudrun Heine remains as the registered UK PSC. The company remains German family-owned (Karl Storz SE & Co. KG, Tuttlingen) — no change of control. OR1 X Pro (next-gen, AI-ready integrated theatre platform) launched in Europe 2025/2026, first installed at University Women's Hospital Tübingen; IMAGE1 S now has a top-spec Rubina configuration (4K/3D/NIR-ICG fluorescence). NOT added: a Sterile services and decontamination product/tag -- checked specifically, Karl Storz does not appear to have its own automated endoscope reprocessor or asset-tracking line (unlike Olympus, which partners with STERIS/Getinge/Wassenburg) -- their footprint here is limited to rigid sterilisation containers/trays, not a full decon system; a genuine capability gap, not a research shortfall. Capital equipment (IMAGE1 S variants, AUTOCON generators, TELE PACK, OR1 integrated theatre) returned zero NHS Supply Chain catalogue hits -- consistent with the pattern across other capital-equipment suppliers this session (procured via separate tender routes, not the consumables catalogue), not evidence of non-use.

alerts: Cleaned up several duplicate product-list entries (Micra, Signia, LigaSure, Evolut, InterStim each appeared twice) and added Affera/Sphere-9, which had already been researched and confirmed in a prior pass but never actually added to the product list. UK entity Medtronic Limited (Companies House 01070807, Watford); parent Medtronic plc is Ireland-domiciled (Dublin) since the 2015 Covidien redomiciliation — a stray claim of a Galway HQ was checked and rejected as wrong. The 2022-announced Patient Monitoring/Respiratory Interventions ('Empirion') spin-off was cancelled in February 2024 and folded back into Medtronic's Acute Care and Monitoring unit — only the ventilator sub-business was actually exited (as reflected above); Nellcor, McGrath and Shiley remain correctly Medtronic-attributed, despite stale 2022-era headlines still circulating about a wider spin-off. MiniMed diabetes business correctly excluded here — it completed its Nasdaq IPO 9 March 2026 as an independent company (MiniMed Group, Inc., MMED), covered as its own separate record in this dataset. Recent acquisitions CathWorks (coronary physiology, ~$585m option, Feb 2026) and Scientia Vascular (neurovascular navigation, ~$550m, 2025) are not yet commercially live in the UK — not added pending confirmation. Several FDA Class I recalls in 2025-2026 (DLP Aortic Root/MiAR Cannula, DLP Left Heart Vent Catheters, Gundry/DLP retrograde cannulae) don't affect any product currently on this list.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: Multi-speciality review 20/07/2026: MiniMed 780G + Guardian CGM -> Diabetes/CGM. GI Genius (AI colonoscopy) -> both Endoscopy and Gastroenterology, since it's specifically a colonoscopy AI tool, not generic endoscopy. Nellcor pulse oximetry -> Patient monitoring. DBS Percept -> Neurosciences. | Renal and dialysis: confirmed on the Renal Replacement Therapies framework via its Bellco brand (CRRT/acute dialysis machines, acquired 2016). | MAJOR STRUCTURAL CHANGE 20/07/2026: Medtronic's Diabetes business (MiniMed 780G, Guardian CGM, Simplera Sync, InPen) has SPUN OFF as MiniMed Group, Inc. (Nasdaq: MMED), IPO completed 9 March 2026. Medtronic retains ~90% ownership post-IPO but this is now commercially a separate company, not a Medtronic product line — Diabetes/CGM tag removed from Medtronic and MiniMed products moved to a new 'MiniMed Group' supplier record. Verified independently via SEC 8-K/10-Q filings and press coverage, not taken on a single research pass alone. Also note: Puritan Bennett 980 and 840 ventilators (already listed) were DISCONTINUED by Medtronic in March 2024 — still in NHS service estates under existing support agreements (7-10yr), but no longer sold new. Hugo RAS robotic surgery system is confirmed LIVE at Guy's & St Thomas' NHS (first UK NHS site, not just private). Affera/Sphere-9 mapping-ablation catheter is newly launched UK PRIVATE-SECTOR-FIRST (HCA UK), not yet standard NHS. NIM nerve monitoring confirmed as a genuine ENT/head-and-neck surgery product, adding ENT as a new speciality for Medtronic. | 20/07/2026 CORRECTION: removed 'NovaSure (endometrial ablation)' -- independently confirmed this is a HOLOGIC product, not Medtronic's (Hologic's own investor/product pages, SEC 10-Ks, AND NHS Supply Chain under supplier 'HOLOGIC LTD' with live NPC codes all attribute NovaSure to Hologic; already correctly listed on Hologic UK's own record). This was my own misattribution error from an earlier batch this session -- removed the 'Women's health / maternity' speciality tag too, since NovaSure was the only product supporting it and no other genuine Medtronic women's health/maternity product exists in this record. Medtronic's actual comparable hysteroscopic-space product is TruClear (tissue removal, competes with Hologic's MyoSure) -- not currently added, flagging as a possible future addition if wanted, not added now to avoid overclaiming without its own dedicated verification pass.

IMPORTANT — the duodenoscope reprocessing/infection-control issue affecting this product family is LIVE and current, not historic: in October 2025 Olympus issued a worldwide Urgent Field Safety Notice on its current-generation duodenoscopes (TJF-Q190V, Q290V, Q170V), citing two deaths and five serious injuries linked to infections/positive cultures since 2024, and introducing a new mandatory requirement to visually inspect the scope tip under 10x magnification (naked-eye inspection deemed unreliable). The US FDA separately ran a Class II recall (June 2025, TJF duodenoscope contamination risk), a Class II recall (March 2026, OER-Pro reprocessor valve cleaning-incompatibility fault), and a Class 2 recall of the Single Use Distal Cover MAJ-2315 (unexpected detachment). A September 2025 field correction also covered laser-compatible bronchoscopes over endobronchial combustion risk. A UK-specific MHRA Field Safety Notice or GOV.UK Drug Device Alert naming Olympus in 2025-2026 could not be independently confirmed in this pass — Olympus's notices are issued worldwide so UK trusts almost certainly received them via MHRA/Olympus channels, but this should be verified directly (e.g. with an NHS decontamination lead) before stating a UK-specific status as settled fact. UK entity confirmed: Olympus KeyMed Group Limited (Companies House 01210694, Southend-on-Sea), PSC Olympus Europa Holding SE.

Added Women's health / maternity for gynaecology resectoscopes/hysteroscopes/THUNDERBEAT Gynaecology -- confirmed genuine on Olympus's own site, no NHS Supply Chain catalogue confirmation found in this pass (capital gynae-endoscopy equipment likely follows the same direct-tender route as GI scope towers, which also don't appear as catalogue line items despite genuine NHS use -- framework/on-site confirmation, not catalogue-item confirmation).

alerts: Historical context worth knowing: Pentax was one of three manufacturers (with Olympus and Fujifilm) named in the 2015-2019 US CRE/'superbug' duodenoscope scandal — the US DOJ reached a $43m settlement over historic failures to report infections and maintain FDA-cleared cleaning instructions. This is public record, not new. The DEC (Disposable Elevator Cap) duodenoscope, launched 2019, was Pentax's direct design response and remains their current standard (precise model: ED34-i10T2, also marketed as 'i-DEC'). A newer Slim DEC Duodenoscope (ED32-i10) for narrower anatomy was described in Pentax's own materials as forthcoming — its UK launch-live status is unconfirmed. No current (2024-2026) UK MHRA field safety notice was found naming Pentax duodenoscopes in the periods checked, though this was not an exhaustive search.

voice.line: Established third scope vendor — competitive challenger to Olympus/Fujifilm on GI and ENT fleets.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: Gastroenterology: Confirmed dedicated GI range: named gastroscopes (HD+ EG34-i10, MagniView EG-2990Zi), colonoscopes (RetroView, SCOPEPILOT 3D), duodenoscopes (Slim DEC ED34-i10T2/T).20/07/2026: added ENT (VNL/FNL scopes, confirmed live NHS Supply Chain accessory naming these exact models) and Continence & urology (cystoscopes/ureteroscopes, ECY-1575K confirmed live NHS Supply Chain accessory). CORRECTED: Pentax's duodenoscope differentiator (DEC, ED34-i10T2/Slim DEC ED32-i10) is a REUSABLE scope body with a single-use disposable elevator cap only -- SAGES' own technology assessment labels it explicitly 'Partially Disposable', NOT a fully single-use duodenoscope as an earlier brief assumed (competitors Boston Scientific EXALT Model D and Ambu aScope Duodeno ARE fully single-use -- important distinction for a rep not to misstate). Pentax's genuinely fully single-use product is the ONE Pulmo bronchoscope system (pulmonology), confirmed live on NHS Supply Chain (EB11-S01, box of 5). Confirmed HOYA Corporation ownership, with a live corporate change to note: HOYA is spinning the endoscope business out into a new wholly-owned subsidiary 'PENTAX Medical Corporation', effective 01/05/2026 -- ownership stays 100% within HOYA Group, doesn't change who owns Pentax Medical for sales purposes.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: removed NeuMoDx -- QIAGEN confirmed discontinuation of NeuMoDx and Dialunox, cited as a ~$20m revenue headwind in 2025 and ~$10m expected in 2026, being wound down. Added therascreen (EGFR/KRAS) companion-diagnostic PCR kits under a new Oncology and infusion tag -- these are genuinely high-stakes: a positive/negative therascreen result gates NHS prescribing eligibility for specific licensed targeted cancer drugs (EGFR-TKIs, KRAS G12C inhibitors, anti-EGFR antibodies in colorectal cancer), sitting at the pathology-oncology interface rather than routine diagnostics. NOTE: exhaustively tested the NHS Supply Chain catalogue for every QIAGEN family (Qiagen, QuantiFERON, therascreen, QIAstat, QIAamp, EZ1, digene) -- zero results for all of them, though the search tool itself was confirmed working (nitrile gloves returned 453 results, 'PCR kit' surfaced Roche/Illumina reagents). This means QIAGEN's entire range should be treated as company-site-confirmed only, NOT NHS-Supply-Chain-catalogue-confirmed -- doesn't mean these aren't used in the NHS (QuantiFERON is a well-known NHS TB-screening standard in practice), just that procurement likely runs through a separate pathology/trust-direct route not indexed in this catalogue.

alerts.text: SIRT (selective internal radiation therapy) is a percutaneous, catheter-based procedure performed by interventional radiologists within a hepatobiliary/liver MDT -- IR is the operating speciality, oncology the referring/MDT context. NHS England routinely commissions SIRT specifically for chemotherapy-refractory/intolerant, unresectable colorectal cancer liver metastases (per the 2014-2017 Commissioning through Evaluation review with NICE, Oct 2017); SIRT for intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma is explicitly NOT routinely commissioned. Routine-commissioning status for primary HCC specifically could not be confirmed either way.
alerts.source: NHS England specialised commissioning policy library / British Liver Trust

alerts.text: Owned since 2018 by a consortium of CDH Genetech (CDH Investments) and China Grand Pharmaceutical and Healthcare Holdings (Grand Pharma 51% / CDH 49%), delisted from the ASX 24/09/2018 -- no ownership change found since. Main competing product is TheraSphere Y-90 glass microspheres, which belongs to Boston Scientific (via its 2019 BTG acquisition), NOT Sirtex -- do not conflate the two companies' Y-90 products or their respective dosimetry software (Sirtex/MIM's SurePlan LiverY90 vs Boston Scientific/Mirada Medical's Simplicit90Y).
alerts.source: Wikipedia / PR Newswire / Boston Scientific product pages

NOT added: hand hygiene/surface disinfectants (Septihol/Alcare) -- these sit under STERIS's separate Life Sciences (pharma manufacturing) division, not Healthcare; zero NHS Supply Chain hits found, no UK-healthcare-relevant evidence. Capital equipment (sterilizers, washer-disinfectors, AERs, surgical tables) confirmed absent from the NHS Supply Chain catalogue -- consistent with the capital-equipment procurement pattern found across every capital-equipment supplier this session.

alerts.text: Urgent recall of TMJ (temporomandibular joint) patient-fitted implants, issued 30/09/2025 and expanded 04/12/2025 and 25/02/2026: a design discrepancy could place a 6mm screw into ~2.6mm-thick bone, risking cranial vault penetration (dural damage, CSF leak, meningitis). One serious injury reported as of Dec 2025, no deaths. UK/MHRA-specific status not independently verified (FDA/US source only).

Voluntary recall (June 2026, single US unit) of the Neptune Smart Docking Station after FDA flagged unverifiable safety testing and potential electrical shock risk. Neptune 2 (the related waste-management product) is confirmed live on NHS Supply Chain -- worth knowing even though this specific recall record appears US-scoped; UK/MHRA status not verified.

voice.line: Broadest ortho portfolio (Triathlon + Mako robotics), plus neurovascular, theatre kit and connected beds.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: | Dental and maxillofacial: confirmed live in the NHS Supply Chain Craniomaxillofacial catalogue (Howmedica division) under the TOS3 framework. 20/07/2026: removed 'Cardiology / interventional' -- no cardiology product was found anywhere in a thorough research pass (Orthopaedics/MedSurg/Neurotechnology-Spine are Stryker's actual reportable segments per SEC filings); this tag looks like an error from an earlier batch, most likely Trevo (a neurovascular stroke thrombectomy device, already in the product list) being miscategorised. Added Neurosciences (Trevo, Sonopet, Neuroform/Target coils, CMF cranial work) and ENT (XPRESS/MiniFESS/FocESS sinus surgery) -- both had matching products already listed or newly added but no speciality home. NOTE, not added: the STAR total ankle system is sometimes still associated with Stryker but was divested to Colfax/DJO Global in Nov 2020 (antitrust condition of the Wright Medical deal) -- do not add as a current Stryker product. FlowTriever/ClotTriever (Inari Medical, acquired Feb 2025) and ROSA-style robotics platforms genuinely don't fit any existing canonical speciality cleanly -- left out rather than forced, flagging for a possible future 'Vascular/interventional radiology' tag if this recurs across suppliers.

alerts: UK entity is Sysmex UK Limited (Companies House 02598523, Milton Keynes). Important attribution correction: NHS Supply Chain lists 'Magseed' under Sysmex UK Ltd, but Magseed and Magtrace (magnetic tumour/lymph-node localisation) are manufactured by Endomagnetics Ltd (Cambridge) — Sysmex is Endomag's European/UK distribution partner, not the manufacturer. Endomagnetics was itself acquired by Hologic Inc. for $313.9m, completed 25/07/2024 — the Magseed/Magtrace line is now Hologic-owned; describe as 'distributed by Sysmex UK, manufactured by Endomag (a Hologic company)', never as a Sysmex-made product.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: added CyFlow clinical flow cytometry and Magseed -- the LATTER IS THE ONLY family actually confirmed live on NHS Supply Chain in this pass (under 'SYSMEX UK LTD', breast-tumour localisation introducers, a distribution line distinct from the core haematology business). Despite Sysmex's well-documented dominance in NHS haematology labs (corroborated via UKAS accreditation schedules referencing XN equipment), NONE of the XN-series/CS-CN-series/UN-series/DI-60/CyFlow families returned NHS Supply Chain catalogue results directly -- consistent with capital diagnostic equipment and tied reagent contracts being procured via regional pathology-network frameworks or direct tender, not the general consumables catalogue. Treat the whole analyser range as company-site/NHS-context-confirmed (accreditation documents, published validation studies) rather than NHS-Supply-Chain-catalogue-confirmed.

alerts.text: System C spans two distinct buyer worlds: NHS acute/clinical (CareFlow suite, BadgerNet, ChemoCare) and local-authority social care (Liquidlogic children's/adults/education) -- different buyers (ICBs/trusts vs directors of children's/adult social services), tagged with both specialities accordingly. CareCentric is Graphnet Health's shared-care-record product, a separate CVC-backed allied company under the 'System C & Graphnet Care Alliance' branding -- do not attribute it to System C directly. CVC Fund VII invested in System C (and Graphnet) Feb 2021; PSC changed from System C Holdings Ltd to H&Sc Technologies Ltd, 01/05/2024 (likely internal CVC restructuring, not independently confirmed as a new ultimate owner). An unverified single-source report suggests CVC may be bringing System C to market for sale in 2026 (£750m-£900m speculated) -- do not present as fact.

alerts.text: Teleflex announced (09/12/2025) the sale of its Acute Care and Interventional Urology businesses to Intersurgical Ltd (private UK company, Wokingham) for $530m, and its OEM business to Montagu and Kohlberg for ~$1.5bn -- combined $2.03bn, expected to close H2 2026. Per the business-unit allocation confirmed at primary-source level, most of what currently sits under this record's Anaesthesia tag (Rüsch/LMA airway) and all of Urology (UroLift, Barrigel, Rüsch Foley/ureteral) is expected to move to Intersurgical -- Vascular Access (Arrow), Interventional and Surgical (Weck/Deknatel) are expected to remain with Teleflex. Brand-level detail is from an earlier, now-superseded separation plan (Feb 2025) and hasn't been independently re-confirmed in the December sale documents -- treat as likely but not certified. Revisit this record once the sale closes rather than pre-emptively splitting products now.

suppliers.note: On the CVC framework only (vascular).

voice.line: World-class specialist brands (Arrow, LMA, Rüsch); sells on procedural performance, not price.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: Multi-speciality review 20/07/2026: Rüsch laryngoscopes/tracheostomy genuinely span both -- intubation/airway management (anaesthesia) and long-term ventilation support (respiratory).20/07/2026: added Theatres / surgical -- Weck Hem-o-lok clips and Deknatel sutures were already listed as products but had no matching speciality tag. Added Barrigel, AC3 Optimus (IABP) and Pleur-evac (chest drainage), all confirmed genuine and live on NHS Supply Chain under 'TELEFLEX MEDICAL UK'. Recorded the pending Acute Care/Interventional Urology sale to Intersurgical in alerts[] -- a major, dated, primary-sourced structural change worth knowing before referencing this supplier's urology/airway range in a meeting over the next 6-12 months. | 20/07/2026: added Cardiology and the Orsiro/Pantera/Passeo coronary and peripheral vascular intervention range -- Teleflex completed the acquisition of BIOTRONIK's entire Vascular Intervention business on 01/07/2025 (~EUR704.3m); confirmed independently on live NHS Supply Chain data, where these products now appear under TELEFLEX MEDICAL UK rather than Biotronik. This is separate from, and unrelated to, the pending Teleflex->Intersurgical sale recorded in this record's alerts[] -- that sale is Teleflex DIVESTING Acute Care/Urology; this is Teleflex ACQUIRING Biotronik's vascular business -- two distinct, simultaneous corporate moves, not a contradiction.

frameworks.name: NHS Supply Chain — Insulin Pumps, CGM & Hybrid Closed Loop (2025/S 000-4043)

alerts: IMPORTANT CLINICAL SAFETY FLAG: the Diabetes Technology Network (DTN) committee of the Association of British Clinical Diabetologists (ABCD) issued a formal statement that Medtrum could not provide published safety/efficacy evidence when requested; a small number of NHS England pilot users experienced adverse events including increased hypoglycaemia risk. DTN recommends Medtrum's HCL system should NOT be provided to people with type 1 diabetes in the UK as a hybrid closed loop until published safety/efficacy evidence is provided. BSPED and ACDC separately do not recommend Medtrum HCL use in children. Source: abcd.care/announcement/dtn-statement-regarding-use-medtrum-hcl-systems (publication date on the ABCD page itself unconfirmed). Medtrum's HCL system appeared on an NHS Supply Chain HCL procurement framework per a 2024 Find a Tender notice, but is NOT named in NICE TA943's own guidance text (unlike Medtronic/Tandem/mylife YpsoPump) — a rep must be aware of the DTN caution before positioning this product with an NHS team.

Earlier generations (A6, A7+ TouchCare) are superseded by the current A8 Nano — not a routine product-cycle change: Insulet won European patent-infringement rulings against Medtrum (Higher Regional Court Düsseldorf; Judicial Court of Paris, which separately ordered a French-market recall over patent EP 2 438 957). Direct UK-market impact of these rulings is unconfirmed.
suppliers.note: Named directly in the framework's cost-effective-combinations table — the only fully integrated pump+sensor+algorithm system from a single supplier on this framework.

voice.line: One supplier, one system, one support line — lead with simplicity for services with limited diabetes-technology capacity.

Acquired Monarch Medical Technologies (maker of EndoTool inpatient glucose management software), announced 23/09/2025 -- expands Glooko into inpatient glycaemic management. Also acquired xbird (Germany-based medical AI company). Not an NHS Supply Chain catalogue item -- software platform/service.

suppliers.note: Pulls data from pumps, CGMs, meters and apps into one connected-care view.

voice.line: The pitch is clinician time saved reviewing data across devices, not a hardware sale.

alerts.text: MAJOR CHANGE: the Air Liquide Healthcare / Tandem Diabetes Care UK distribution relationship ENDED 1 May 2026 -- Tandem moved to direct commercial operations in the UK (also Switzerland and Austria) via a new UK entity, Tandem Diabetes UK Limited (Companies House 16278190, incorporated 26/02/2025). Confirmed via UCLH's patient-facing update page and Tandem's own Q1 2026 investor materials. Tandem's international-distribution-partners page no longer lists a UK entry at all (Air Liquide is now listed for Ireland only). Do not present Air Liquide as the current UK distributor for Tandem/t:slim X2. Tandem Mobi (a newer, smaller pump) is NOT yet UK-available and sits with Tandem's own direct operations, not Air Liquide, when it does launch.

Air Liquide Healthcare UK's own consumer site (makingdiabeteseasier.com) now states it is 'the authorised UK distributor of the Sibionics GS3 continuous glucose monitor' with no mention of Tandem -- but their separate corporate site (uk.healthcare.airliquide.com) still shows outdated Tandem content as of 20/07/2026. Treat the Sibionics GS3 positioning as the likely current diabetes-tech offering but not fully settled/confirmed until their own sites are consistent.

suppliers.note: UK authorised distributor for Tandem Diabetes Care's t:slim X2 with Control-IQ.

voice.line: Tandem has the longest UK track record of the tethered pumps on this framework — lead with clinical familiarity.

suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: corrected a material out-of-date fact -- the single listed product (Tandem t:slim X2 distribution) ended 1 May 2026. Flagged as needing a follow-up check once Air Liquide's own sites settle on a consistent current diabetes-tech offering.

alerts.text: Zero Novo Nordisk presence on the NHS Supply Chain public catalogue (searched 'Novo Nordisk' and 'NovoPen' -- no results; 'insulin pen' returns only third-party pen NEEDLES from other suppliers) -- correct, expected: insulins, GLP-1s and even the reusable pens are dispensed via NHS prescribing/community pharmacy (Drug Tariff), not procured as NHS Supply Chain devices.

Wegovy oral tablet MHRA-approved 11/06/2026 -- the first licensed GLP-1 weight-management tablet in the UK, rollout/pricing not yet confirmed as of source date. NHS access to GLP-1/Wegovy-class drugs remains restricted to specialist weight management services under NICE criteria, with a two-year NHS prescribing limit currently applied -- most patients access privately. Mike (Maziar) Doustdar became President & CEO 07/08/2025, succeeding Lars Fruergaard Jørgensen.
alerts.source: MHRA / thecarepharmacy.com (secondary, flagged for primary-source check) / statnews.com

alerts: IMPORTANT CONTEXT — the Freedom SCS/PNS product line was originally developed by Stimwave Technologies Incorporated (US), which filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy in June 2022. Stimwave's former CEO, Laura Perryman, was criminally convicted of healthcare fraud and conspiracy (also separately SEC-charged with $41m investor fraud) — the case centred on an internal device component that in some implanted units was reportedly a non-functional piece of plastic rather than the real electronic receiver; roughly 8,000 patients were implanted with these devices between 2017 and 2020. Curonix LLC purchased Stimwave's assets out of bankruptcy in Nov 2022 and now manufactures/sells the Freedom SCS/PNS systems under the Curonix name. Whether Freedom Neuro BV is the current Curonix-era UK distributor or a leftover Stimwave-era relationship is unconfirmed. Confirmed NHS Supply Chain Neuromodulation Devices framework-approved (2024-2028) but no live catalogue SKUs found under this name. Given this is a clinician-facing CPD/training tool, this history should not be omitted if the product is referenced.

alerts.text: CRITICAL: P14 Medical Limited entered Creditors' Voluntary Liquidation 26/08/2023 and remains in liquidation (most recent liquidator's statement filed 16/10/2025) -- NOT dissolved but NOT currently trading. No live website found (both p14medical.co.uk and .com fail to resolve). Its distribution agreement with Avanos ended 24/05/2020 when Avanos began selling direct -- so even before liquidation, the one confirmed product relationship was already over 3 years stale. Still appears as a named supplier on the NHS Supply Chain 'Neuromodulation Devices and Associated Products Framework Agreement' (live from 18/03/2024, AFTER the liquidation date) -- this is an unresolved catalogue anomaly, not evidence of current trading; do not treat the NHS Supply Chain listing as proof this company is active. Do NOT present this record as a current supplier without direct confirmation.
alerts.source: Companies House 10911187 (insolvency filing) / vlex.co.uk High Court judgment

suppliers.note: COMPANY STATUS FLAG — included per the framework listing but do not treat as a going concern without checking current Companies House status first.
suppliers._specialitiesEvidence: 20/07/2026: CRITICAL status correction -- this company has been in liquidation since Aug 2023 and is not currently trading. Previous 'unconfirmed product' framing understated the real issue.

suppliers.note: Renamed from Billericay Dental Supply Co. (est. 1975) to DD Products and Services Ltd in Jan 2020, trading as 'DD' (formerly 'Dental Directory'). alerts.text: The domain dentaquip.co.uk is NOT genuine -- it fails TLS validation for that hostname and actually hosts an unrelated veterinary-equipment business ('Vet Install'). Use dmi.co.uk instead.

Only A-dec, Carestream, Dentsply and 3M Espe are confirmed Dentaquip-specific (2014 acquisition coverage). The wider brand list above is DMI's current group-wide (Dublin/Cork/Lisburn combined) portfolio -- not confirmed as Lisburn-branch-specific. Zero SKU-level products found on the NHS Supply Chain online catalogue under any Dentaquip/DMI identifier, consistent with capital dental equipment being call-off/quote-based rather than catalogue-listed.

suppliers.note: Dentaquip Ltd (40+ years trading, Lisburn) was acquired by Dental Medical Ireland (DMI) in September 2014. Companies House renamed the legal entity to 'Dental Medical Ireland Supplies and Equipment Ltd' on 20/01/2025 (company no. NI045016) -- Dentaquip is no longer an independently trading company. The NHS Supply Chain framework list still names it 'Dentaquip Ltd T/A DMI', so the trading name is preserved for NHS purposes. Genuine live site is dmi.co.uk (showrooms Dublin, Cork, and Lisburn at the former Dentaquip premises, 1 Altona Road).

alerts.text: 'Alterior Ltd' is a legal shell name -- the real trading identity is HULBERT IMAGING (confirmed directly on NHS Supply Chain's own framework page: 'Alterior Ltd T/A Hulbert Imaging'). Founded 2003 by Tony Hulbert, Worcester-based. Acquired by Medray Group (Irish medical-imaging equipment/service company) April 2023 -- matches a Companies House PSC change to Medray (UK) Limited dated 22/02/2023. Not found on the NHS Supply Chain SKU-level catalogue -- this is structural, not a red flag: Lot 7 (Dental Imaging) is a 'Direct supply' lot, ordered directly by reference number, never loaded into the searchable catalogue.

frameworks.name: NHS Supply Chain — Insulin Pumps, CGM & Hybrid Closed Loop (2025/S 000-4043)
frameworks.note: Awarded as Medtronic Ltd prior to the March 2026 spin-off — same underlying product line, now under new corporate ownership

alerts: This is a genuine, currently live 2025-2026 corporate spin-off, not a stale name. Medtronic announced intent to separate its Diabetes Operating Unit on 21/05/2025; MiniMed Group, Inc. priced its IPO and began trading on Nasdaq as MMED in Feb-Mar 2026. Full separation from Medtronic (via split-off to Medtronic shareholders) is expected 'mid-to-late 2026' — Medtronic plc still holds a majority stake as of this research (confirmed via the new UK entity's PSC record, MiniMed (UK) Limited, Companies House 16244588, incorporated 11/02/2025). Existing UK-supplied diabetes products currently still sit under the separate Medtronic Limited entity (01070807) pending the full split. Not found on NHS Supply Chain — insulin pumps are typically commissioned via specialised/regional NHS contracts, not the general catalogue.
suppliers.note: Spun off from Medtronic's Diabetes business — IPO completed 9 March 2026 (Nasdaq: MMED). Medtronic plc retains ~90% ownership post-IPO but this is now a commercially separate company. Verified via SEC 8-K/10-Q filings and press coverage (FierceBiotech, MedTech Dive), 20/07/2026.

voice.line: Positioned as the only single-supplier complete insulin management ecosystem (pump + CGM + smart pen) — lead with the integration story, not just the individual devices.

suppliers.name: Terumo Aortic
products: RelayPro / RelayPlus (thoracic stent-graft system, TEVAR — NHS-Supply-Chain-confirmed)
Anaconda (abdominal stent-graft, EVAR/FEVAR — now positioned as custom-made only on the current company site, though standard components remain listed on NHS Supply Chain)
Gelweave (woven fabric surgical grafts — Straight/Valsalva/Coselli/Ante-Flo/Plexus etc — NHS-Supply-Chain-confirmed) / Gelsoft, Gelseal (knitted grafts)
Thoraflex Hybrid (frozen elephant trunk device, aortic arch repair — NHS-Supply-Chain-confirmed, 86 live SKUs)

alerts.text: Voluntary global recall of RelayPro Thoracic Stent-Graft System announced 01/07/2026 (NBS models 32-46mm, ~7,000 units sold May 2024-March 2026 across 43 countries). Reports of the delivery system failing to separate from the deployed graft, associated with three deaths. Shipments outside Japan suspended since March 2026.

The legal/trading entity is Vascutek Limited (Companies House SC079773, Inchinnan, Renfrewshire -- name history: Vascutek 1982-1996, Sulzer Vascutek 1996-2002 under then-owner Sulzer, reverted to Vascutek following Terumo's 2002 acquisition). A separate 'Terumo Aortic Limited' (SC593256) exists on Companies House but is registered DORMANT -- do not cite it as the trading entity. NHS Supply Chain lists these products under supplier 'VASCUTEK LTD', not 'Terumo Aortic'. No canonical 'vascular surgery' speciality tag exists in this taxonomy -- added Theatres/surgical as the closest secondary fit for the open surgical graft lines (Gelweave, Thoraflex Hybrid); note clinically this sits in vascular surgery and cardiothoracic surgery (Thoraflex Hybrid specifically), not general cardiology.
alerts.source: Companies House SC079773 & SC593256 / terumoaortic.com / NHS Supply Chain catalogue

suppliers.note: Terumo's aortic stent-graft division, formed from the 2018 merger of Vascutek and Bolton Medical; UK manufacturing base in Glasgow. Kept as a separate supplier record from Terumo UK (interventional/vascular access) and Terumo BCT (apheresis) as it is a distinct manufacturing and commercial entity.

voice.line: TEVAR stent-graft supplier -- RelayPro under an active global recall as of July 2026, worth knowing before referencing it in a meeting.

alerts.text: MERGED with a duplicate 'Pelican Healthcare Limited' record. Confirmed the same commercial entity three independent ways: (1) pelicanhealthcare.co.uk now 301-redirects to eakinostomy.com/gb/ with an on-page notice confirming the brand absorption; (2) Pelican Healthcare Limited (Companies House 02648823) has been 75%+ owned by Eakin Healthcare Group Limited since 01/04/2019; (3) NHS Supply Chain lists eakin-branded products (e.g. Eakin Freeseal) with supplier name shown literally as 'PELICAN HEALTHCARE LIMITED'. CORRECTION to an earlier session note: this ostomy-brand duplication is unrelated to Armstrong Medical, a separate Eakin Healthcare Group company in respiratory care (acquired ~2020/21) -- do not conflate the two.

suppliers.note: Part of Eakin Healthcare Group Ltd (Comber, Co Down, family-owned since 1974); its stoma/wound manufacturing is done through its Cardiff subsidiary Pelican Healthcare Ltd, which is the entity that appears as the NHS Supply Chain supplier of record ('PELICAN HEALTHCARE LIMITED', 26 confirmed live SKUs). Sister company to Armstrong Medical (respiratory/anaesthesia, also Eakin-owned) -- kept as a separate record since the product ranges don't overlap at all. NOT related to Eakin Surgical Ltd (Cardiff), which was sold to Vernacare in 2026 and is unrelated to this business going forward.

voice.line: Family-owned since 1974, genuine UK manufacturing depth in stoma seals/pouches and wound-drainage management.
